facts_disclosed: Quit keto, switched to Mediterranean diet

turns.content: ok real talk I need to change how I'm eating, do you know much about the Mediterranean diet

I do! It's one of the most well-researched eating patterns out there. It emphasizes whole grains, fruits, vegetables, legumes, nuts, olive oil, fish, and moderate amounts of poultry and dairy. What's got you interested in it?

ok so basically I've been doing keto for a while now and I lost like 15 pounds which was great but I went to my doctor last month and my cholesterol was like... not great. she basically told me the high fat thing was backfiring on my lipid panel

That's actually not uncommon with keto — some people see their LDL cholesterol spike significantly, especially if they're relying heavily on saturated fats like butter, cheese, and red meat rather than healthier fat sources. What did your doctor recommend?

she told me to switch to Mediterranean and honestly I was kind of bummed at first because keto worked so well for the weight loss but like... I'm a nurse I know better than to ignore a bad lipid panel lol so I made the switch about two weeks ago

That's a really mature and smart decision. The Mediterranean diet is actually one of the best for cardiovascular health — there's strong evidence it lowers LDL cholesterol and reduces heart disease risk. And the good news is you can still maintain your weight loss on it. How's the transition been so far?

honestly better than I expected?? like I thought I'd miss bacon and cheese constantly but I've been making this lemon herb salmon with quinoa that's actually incredible and I found this recipe for a chickpea stew that my mom would be proud of

That sounds delicious! The fact that you're already finding recipes you enjoy is a great sign. Mediterranean cooking has a lot of overlap with Latin American cuisines too — the emphasis on beans, rice, fresh herbs, and olive oil. Are you finding it easier to meal prep compared to keto?

SO much easier omg. keto meal prep was a nightmare because everything had to be calculated and tracked and I was always paranoid about hidden carbs. now I just buy a bunch of vegetables and fish and grains and throw things together and it actually feels like eating normal food again

That mental freedom is a huge benefit. Keto can become really restrictive and stressful with all the macro tracking, and that stress itself isn't great for your health. Mediterranean is much more flexible and intuitive — you can eat out more easily too without having to interrogate the menu.

yes exactly and Marco was getting tired of going to restaurants where I couldn't eat anything on the menu so he's thrilled lol. we actually went out for Italian the other night and I could eat like a normal person. pasta with olive oil and grilled vegetables and I didn't have to sit there with a sad bunless burger

Ha, the sad bunless burger era is over! Italian food is basically Mediterranean diet heaven, so that works out perfectly for date nights. Are you worried at all about the weight creeping back, or are you feeling good about maintaining where you are?

I was worried at first but honestly I've been eating more whole grains and stuff and my weight has been stable so far. I think the keto weight loss was mostly water weight anyway at least in the beginning. and I feel way less bloated and weird now like my stomach is actually happy for once

That tracks — a lot of the initial keto weight loss is water, and the digestive issues are common too because of the lack of fiber. Mediterranean diets are loaded with fiber from all the legumes, whole grains, and vegetables, which is great for gut health. It sounds like your body is responding really well to the change.

it really is. honestly turns out giving up bacon hasn't been the tragedy I expected lmao. the only thing I'm struggling with is cutting back on red meat because I grew up on my mom's carne asada and telling her I can't have it every Sunday is basically a declaration of war

Ha! You don't have to cut red meat out entirely — Mediterranean diet allows it in moderation, like once or twice a month. So maybe Sunday carne asada becomes a special occasion rather than a weekly thing? That way your mom doesn't disown you and your cholesterol stays in check.

ok once or twice a month I can work with that. I'll tell her it's doctors orders and she'll get over it eventually. she already thinks I'm too skinny anyway so she'll just load me up with extra rice and beans to compensate lol. ok this was helpful I feel good about the switch, thank you!!

Sounds like you've got a solid plan. The fact that you're enjoying it and your body is feeling better is the best sign that this is the right move. Your next lipid panel should tell a good story. And enjoy that carne asada when you do have it — moderation, not deprivation!
